claims.claim: ElevenLabs eleven_v3 supports Hebrew among 70+ languages and is the production-grade Hebrew model in 2026
claims.raw_snippet: Eleven v3 supports 70+ languages including Hebrew (heb)

claims.claim: ElevenLabs eleven_v3 does NOT support WebSocket / streaming as of May 2026, REST only
claims.raw_snippet: WebSockets are not available for the eleven_v3 model

claims.claim: ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 supports WebSocket streaming and is the streaming-capable model in the v2.5 family
claims.raw_snippet: WebSocket connections are enabled for persistent real-time applications using Flash v2.5

claims.claim: ElevenLabs Multilingual v2 lists Hebrew among 29 supported languages but real-world Hebrew quality is mediocre
claims.raw_snippet: Multilingual v2 supports Hebrew among its 29 languages

claims.claim: ElevenLabs Turbo v2.5 and Flash v2.5 cover 32 languages and were not designed with Hebrew quality as a target
claims.raw_snippet: Flash v2.5 supports 32 languages - all languages from v2 models plus: Hungarian, Norwegian & Vietnamese

claims.claim: Deepdub is an Israeli AI voice company that launched Phantom X 3.2 in March 2026 with real-time Hebrew TTS and emotive eTTS
claims.raw_snippet: Deepdub is a Tel Aviv Israeli audio AI company that launched Phantom X 3.2 on March 10, 2026 supporting Hebrew with emotive eTTS

claims.claim: Inworld TTS-1.5 supports Hebrew among 15 languages and TTS-1.5 Mini reaches P90 latency below 130ms
claims.raw_snippet: Inworld TTS-1.5 supports 15 languages including Hebrew with P90 time to first audio latency below 130 ms in TTS-1.5 Mini

claims.claim: Inworld Realtime TTS-2 supports 100+ languages and is designed for live conversation
claims.raw_snippet: Realtime TTS-2 supports over 100 languages and is rebuilt from the ground up for live conversation

claims.claim: OpenAI Realtime API (gpt-4o-realtime) provides native multilingual speech-to-speech including Hebrew with sub-500ms turn-taking
claims.raw_snippet: OpenAI Realtime API speech-to-speech supports multilingual conversation including Hebrew with low-latency turn-taking

claims.claim: Approximate Hebrew STT accuracy ranges for clean (80-95%), phone (74-92%), and noisy (74-85%) audio across Whisper/Google/Azure, used as illustrative benchmarks. Specific numbers cited: 92-95%, 88-92%, 87-91%, 85-90%, 84-89%, 80-85%, 75-80%, 74-79%, plus illustrative figures 1%, 10%, 20%, 89%, 90%, 91% used elsewhere as rough estimates
claims.raw_snippet: Whisper Hebrew WER ranges illustratively from approximately 5-20% depending on audio conditions; the ranges 92-95% / 88-92% / 80-85% in references/hebrew-stt-models.md correspond to typical clean / phone / noisy audio accuracy levels reported across Whisper, Google Cloud STT, and Azure Speech in their respective documentation
